_metadata.description: 2017 fatality locations on the Hopkins corridor
_metadata.source: Berkeley City Council budget referral, January 2018
_metadata.note: These two fatalities triggered the Hopkins Street corridor study and its eventual designation as a High-Injury Street in Berkeley's Vision Zero Action Plan (2020).

properties.description: Pedestrian struck at Hopkins and Monterey Avenue intersection
properties.source: Berkeley City Council budget referral, January 2018
properties.note: East of project corridor boundary (Sutter Ave). Included because it directly motivated the corridor study.

properties.description: Cyclist fatality on Sacramento Avenue near Hopkins Street
properties.source: Berkeley City Council budget referral, January 2018
properties.note: Within project corridor. Sacramento Ave bisects the commercial strip — the most contested segment.
